ig_archived_post_media.title:
Chicken Kiev Special $26
300g House Made, Bone in, Garlic Butter, with Cos Salad

Chicken Kiev is probably Russian, not Ukrainian, in origin. It most likely finds its beginnings in 18th- and early 19th-century Russian preference for French style cooking. One source says it was invented by Nicolas Appert, a Frenchman, and chef to the Empress Elizabeth Petrovna (d.1762). Some claim that Chicken Kiev is a modern invention; at any rate, the popularity of the dish in Russian restaurants soared in the middle of the 20th Century.

OUTTASPACE WEEKENDER is a series of shows featuring bands and mates on the label.

OUTTASPACE PRESENTS... the finest selection of space junk from around the country, sifted and graded in order of GARAGE, PUNK, ROCKâNâROLL, FUZZ, COUNTRY, METAL, PSYCH and INDIE. We pick the best, most happening, underground and out-of-control artists and implant them on SMALL-RUN VINYL. Far-out and way-out tracks for the discerning human, OUTTASPACE is an Australian record label that puts artists first so you can add to your SONIC UNIVERSE in tremendous, SPLENDID stereo!

Bar Shout Industry Night | Mon 27th May 

The crew over the road at @shoutbrewing have an epic little op to gain some knowledge about one of our favourite things...BEER! 

Kicking off with Beer 101 with Jake the brewer, you'll get to check out the brewery, see the breakdown of malts and hops, learn about the ins and outs of brewing beer and enjoy a complimentary tasting session. 

Following this, we'll be putting on a Bar Shout at the Oak from 7 pm. Which means if you missed out on a spot for 'Beer 101' you can still come and enjoy a few delicious Mullet Pale Ales. 

To RSVP for 'Beer 101' email your name and the venue you work for to sales@shoutbrewing.com.au

POOR KELLY | THURS 21ST DEC | 7 PM | OUR JOINT

Poor Kelly returns for Newcastle's second annual Gravy Day gig, a celebration of Paul Kelly's music, Christmas, friends, and of course, gravy and the making of it. 

Tired of mediocrity, local singer-songwriter Michael McDonagh (Remember the Circus) pulled together an all-star band and a new suit for last year's event, held in the infamous Fern Street backyard. 

This year it's the same suit, but The Oak in Tighes Hill are the perfect new hosts. Come along and have a peek at their new fit out or enjoy a very special Gravy Day roast dinner.

The all star band are back, and will be supported this year by a Crowded Hour of Power. That is, of course, (approximately) one hour of Crowded House belters to get you very warm indeed. 

Come for a dance, a singalong, great food, cold beer, heck, maybe even some Christmas cheer. 

It's an expensive ol' month, but thanks to The Oak, entry is FREE and all are welcome.
So get along early and enjoy yourself, with entertainment scheduled from 7pm. 

Some of Newcastle's finest musicians will be there to help you kick off the festive season right. You're not going to want to miss this one.

@rememberthecircus
